factors.description: No monetary cap on indemnification exposure
One party bears disproportionate indemnification burden

factors.factor: No mutual waiver of consequential damages
factors.description: Consequential damages exposure without limitation
Losses definition includes speculative or remote damages
Pricing increases automatically without ceiling or opt-out
Vendor can change pricing with minimal notice
Accelerated payment timeline creating cash flow pressure

factors.factor: Late payment penalties exceeding market rate
factors.description: Penalties above 1.5% per month or state usury limits
Cannot exit agreement even for material breach
Cure period exceeds 60 days for material breach
All remaining fees become due immediately upon termination
Locked into agreement for full term without exit option

factors.factor: Vendor claims ownership of customer data
factors.description: Agreement assigns data ownership to vendor

factors.factor: No data return obligation on termination
factors.description: No requirement to return or destroy customer data post-term

factors.factor: Broad data usage rights for vendor
factors.description: Vendor may use customer data for analytics, training, or sharing
No specific timeline or process for data breach notification
Counterparty claims ownership of all work product including pre-existing IP
Agreement fails to exclude pre-existing IP from assignment
Assigning party loses all rights to use assigned IP
Contract renews automatically without advance notice to customer
One party can change terms without consent
Conflicts between documents resolved in counterparty favor
No price protection against better deals to competitors
